* feat: update memory stage to remove unnecessary signals

* feat: processor implementation

* fix: lint errors

examples.before_code:
# Variables
ASSEMBLER_SCRIPT = assembler/main.py
MEM_FILE = simulation/memory.mem

# == 1. CONFIGURABLE INPUTS (The ?= means "use this unless user overrides it") ==
# Usage: make asm ASM_SOURCE=assembler/programs/fibonacci.asm
ASM_SOURCE ?= assembler/programs/test_prog.asm

# Usage: make compile COMPILE_SCRIPT=scripts/compile_alu.do
COMPILE_SCRIPT ?= scripts/compile.do

# Usage: make sim SIM_SCRIPT=scripts/run_alu.do
SIM_SCRIPT ?= scripts/run.do

# Tools
PYTHON = python3
VSIM = vsim.exe
VCOM = vcom.exe
VLIB = vlib.exe
VMAP = vmap.exe

# Directories
WORK_DIR = simulation/work

# Default target
all: clean asm lib compile sim

# 1. Run the Assembler
asm:
	@echo "--- Assembling $(ASM_SOURCE) ---"
	$(PYTHON) $(ASSEMBLER_SCRIPT) $(ASM_SOURCE) > $(MEM_FILE)

# 2. Create ModelSim Library
lib:
	@echo "--- Creating Library ---"
	cd simulation && $(VLIB) work
	cd simulation && $(VMAP) work work

# 3. Compile VHDL
# Uses $(COMPILE_SCRIPT) which defaults to "scripts/compile.do"
compile:
	@echo "--- Compiling with $(COMPILE_SCRIPT) ---"
	cd simulation && $(VSIM) -c -do "do $(COMPILE_SCRIPT); quit"

# 4. Run Simulation (GUI Mode)
# Uses $(SIM_SCRIPT) which defaults to "scripts/run.do"
sim:
	@echo "--- Simulating with $(SIM_SCRIPT) ---"
	cd simulation && $(VSIM) -gui -do "do $(SIM_SCRIPT)"

# 5. Clean up ModelSim trash
clean:
	@echo "--- Cleaning ---"
	rm -rf $(WORK_DIR)
	rm -f simulation/transcript
	rm -f simulation/*.wlf
	rm -f $(MEM_FILE)

examples.before_code:
library ieee;
use ieee.std_logic_1164.all;
use work.cpu_defs_pkg.all;

entity hazard_detection_unit is
  port (
    -- Load-use hazard detection (from Decode and Execute stages)
    read_addr_1_decode      : in std_logic_vector(2 downto 0);
    read_addr_2_decode      : in std_logic_vector(2 downto 0);
    write_addr_execute      : in std_logic_vector(2 downto 0);
    mem_read_enable_execute : in std_logic; -- Is Execute stage doing a LOAD?

    -- Jump/Branch detection signals
    jump_taken_from_decode  : in std_logic; -- Unconditional jump (jmp_type = "00" and is_branch)
    jump_taken_from_execute : in std_logic; -- Conditional jump result from Execute
    mem_to_pc_enable        : in std_logic; -- RET/RTI (PC comes from memory)

    -- detection outputs
    stall_fetch   : out std_logic; -- Stall PC update
    stall_decode  : out std_logic; -- Stall IF/ID register
    flush_fetch   : out std_logic; -- Flush instruction in Fetch (IF/ID)
    flush_decode  : out std_logic; -- Flush instruction in Decode (ID/EX)
    flush_execute : out std_logic -- Insert bubble into Execute
  );
end entity hazard_detection_unit;

architecture behavioral of hazard_detection_unit is
  signal load_use_hazard : std_logic;
begin

  -- =============================================
  -- Load-Use Hazard Detection
  -- =============================================
  -- If instruction in Execute is a LOAD (mem_read_enable = '1')
  -- AND the destination register matches a source register in Decode
  -- We need to stall for 1 cycle because the data won't be ready

  load_use_hazard <= '1' when (mem_read_enable_execute = '1') and
    ((read_addr_1_decode = write_addr_execute) or
    (read_addr_2_decode = write_addr_execute))
    else
    '0';

  -- =============================================
  -- Stall Logic (Load-Use Hazard only)
  -- =============================================
  -- When load-use hazard detected:
  -- 1. Stall Fetch (hold PC)
  -- 2. Stall Decode (hold IF/ID register)
  -- 3. Insert bubble (NOP) into Execute

  stall_fetch  <= load_use_hazard;
  stall_decode <= load_use_hazard;

  -- =============================================
  -- Flush Logic
  -- =============================================

  -- Flush Fetch (IF/ID register):
  -- - Unconditional jump from Decode (1 wrong instruction in Fetch)
  -- - Conditional jump from Execute (1 of 2 wrong instructions)
  -- - RET/RTI (PC from memory)
  flush_fetch <= jump_taken_from_decode or
    jump_taken_from_execute or
    mem_to_pc_enable;

  -- Flush Decode (ID/EX register):
  -- - Conditional jump from Execute (2 wrong instructions: Fetch + Decode)
  -- - RET/RTI (2 wrong instructions)
  flush_decode <= jump_taken_from_execute or
    mem_to_pc_enable;

  -- Flush Execute (insert bubble/NOP):
  -- - Load-use hazard (need 1 cycle delay)
  -- - RET/RTI (PC from memory)
  flush_execute <= load_use_hazard or mem_to_pc_enable;

end architecture behavioral;
examples.after_code:
library ieee;
  use ieee.std_logic_1164.all;
  use work.cpu_defs_pkg.all;

entity hazard_detection_unit is
  port (
    -- Load-use hazard detection (from Decode and Execute stages)
    read_addr_1_decode         : in    std_logic_vector(2 downto 0);
    read_addr_2_decode         : in    std_logic_vector(2 downto 0);
    write_addr_execute         : in    std_logic_vector(2 downto 0);
    ex_mem_sp_enable           : in    std_logic;  -- Is Execute stage modifying SP?
    ex_mem_push_pop_select     : in    push_pop_t; -- Is Execute stage doing PUSH/POP?
    mem_read_enable_execute    : in    std_logic;  -- Is Execute stage doing a LOAD?
    mem_write_enable_execute   : in    std_logic;  -- Is Execute stage doing a STORE?
    is_imm_instruction_execute : in    std_logic;  -- Is Execute stage an immediate instruction?

    -- Jump/Branch detection signals
    jump_taken_from_decode  : in    std_logic; -- Unconditional jump (jmp_type = "00" and is_branch)
    jump_taken_from_execute : in    std_logic; -- Conditional jump result from Execute
    mem_to_pc_enable        : in    std_logic; -- RET/RTI (PC comes from memory)

    -- detection outputs
    stall_fetch  : out   std_logic := '0'; -- Stall PC update
    flush_if_id  : out   std_logic := '0'; -- Flush instruction in Fetch (IF/ID)
    flush_id_ex  : out   std_logic := '0'; -- Flush instruction in Decode (ID/EX)
    flush_ex_mem : out   std_logic := '0'  -- Insert bubble into Execute
  );
end entity hazard_detection_unit;

architecture behavioral of hazard_detection_unit is

  signal load_use_hazard : std_logic;

  signal mem_structual_hazard : std_logic;

begin

  -- =============================================
  -- Load-Use Hazard Detection
  -- =============================================
  -- If instruction in Execute is a LOAD (mem_read_enable = '1')
  -- AND the destination register matches a source register in Decode
  -- We need to stall for 1 cycle because the data won't be ready

  load_use_hazard <= '1' when (ex_mem_sp_enable = '1' and ex_mem_push_pop_select = pop_op) and
                              ((read_addr_1_decode = write_addr_execute) or
                       (read_addr_2_decode = write_addr_execute)) else
                     '0';

  mem_structual_hazard <= '1' when (mem_read_enable_execute = '1' or
                                     mem_write_enable_execute = '1') else
                          '0';
  -- =============================================
  -- Stall Logic (Load-Use Hazard only)
  -- =============================================
  -- When load-use hazard detected:
  -- 1. Stall Fetch (hold PC)
  -- 2. Stall Decode (hold IF/ID register)
  -- 3. Insert bubble (NOP) into Execute

  stall_fetch <= load_use_hazard or mem_structual_hazard; -- stalls pc and if/id

  -- =============================================
  -- Flush Logic
  -- =============================================

  -- Flush Fetch (IF/ID register):
  -- - Unconditional jump from Decode (1 wrong instruction in Fetch)
  -- - Conditional jump from Execute (1 of 2 wrong instructions)
  -- - RET/RTI (PC from memory)
  flush_if_id <= jump_taken_from_execute or
                 mem_to_pc_enable;

  -- Flush Decode (ID/EX register):
  -- - Conditional jump from Execute (2 wrong instructions: Fetch + Decode)
  -- - RET/RTI (2 wrong instructions)
  flush_id_ex <= (mem_to_pc_enable or is_imm_instruction_execute or mem_structual_hazard); -- or immediate inst from ex/mem

  -- Flush Execute (insert bubble/NOP):
  -- - Load-use hazard (need 1 cycle delay)
  -- - RET/RTI (PC from memory)
  flush_ex_mem <= load_use_hazard or mem_to_pc_enable;

end architecture behavioral;

   -- == PUSH / POP OPERATIONS ==

examples.commit_message:
feat: integration (#11)

* feat: update memory stage to remove unnecessary signals

* feat: processor implementation

* fix: lint errors

* feat: decode signals modifications to align with buffers, an
examples.after_code:
library ieee;
  use ieee.std_logic_1164.all;
  use ieee.numeric_std.all;
  use work.cpu_defs_pkg.all;

entity decode_stage is
  port (
    clk  : in    std_logic;
    rst  : in    std_logic;
    intr : in    std_logic; -- Hardware Int

    -- inputs from fetch  (IF/ID Register)
    if_id_instruction : in    std_logic_vector(31 downto 0);
    if_id_pc_plus_1   : in    std_logic_vector(31 downto 0);

    -- inputs from data bus (for immediate bypass)
    data_bus_in : in    std_logic_vector(31 downto 0);

    -- inputs from wb stage
    wb_reg_write_en : in    std_logic;
    wb_dest_addr    : in    std_logic_vector(2 downto 0);
    wb_write_data   : in    std_logic_vector(31 downto 0);

    -- Outputs to ID/EX Pipeline Register
    -- data
    id_ex_rdata1    : out   std_logic_vector(31 downto 0); --
    id_ex_rdata2    : out   std_logic_vector(31 downto 0); --
    id_ex_pc_plus_1 : out   std_logic_vector(31 downto 0); --

    -- register addresses for forwarding unit and wb
    id_ex_rs1_addr : out   std_logic_vector(2 downto 0); --
    id_ex_rs2_addr : out   std_logic_vector(2 downto 0); --
    id_ex_rd_addr  : out   std_logic_vector(2 downto 0); -- The selected dest index

    stall_fetch : out   std_logic; -- To Fetch Stage

    -- Execute Controls
    id_ex_alu_op       : out   alu_op_t;         --
    id_ex_ccr_write_en : out   std_logic;        --
    id_ex_ccr_src_sel  : out   ccr_src_select_t; --

    -- Memory Controls
    id_ex_mem_read     : out   std_logic;            --
    id_ex_mem_write    : out   std_logic;            --
    id_ex_sp_enable    : out   std_logic;            --
    id_ex_push_pop     : out   push_pop_t;           --
    id_ex_mem_data_src : out   mem_write_data_src_t; --

    -- WB Controls
    id_ex_reg_write_en : out   std_logic; --
    id_ex_wb_source    : out   wb_src_t;  --
    -- id_ex_ccr_enable   : out   std_logic;--

    -- Branch/Flow Controls
    id_ex_jmp_type         : out   jmp_t;     --
    id_ex_is_branch        : out   std_logic; --
    id_ex_mem_to_pc_enable : out   std_logic; --
    id_ex_is_imm_instr     : out   std_logic; -- for HDU--

    -- I/O Controls
    id_ex_is_out_port : out   std_logic; --
    id_ex_is_in_port  : out   std_logic; --

    immediate : out   std_logic_vector(31 downto 0);

    jmp_take_out           : out   std_logic; -- to fetch stage for branch decision
    is_swap_out : out   std_logic
  );
end entity decode_stage;

architecture structural of decode_stage is

  signal s_opcode : opcode_t; -- first 5 bits

  -- Instruction Fields
  signal s_rdst_idx  : std_logic_vector(2 downto 0);  -- Bits 26 downto 24
  signal s_rsrc1_idx : std_logic_vector(2 downto 0);  -- Bits 23 downto 21
  signal s_rsrc2_idx : std_logic_vector(2 downto 0);  -- Bits 20 downto 18
  -- signal s_imm_16    : std_logic_vector(15 downto 0); -- Bits 15 downto 0

  -- Control Unit Outputs
  signal cu_reg_write_sel : std_logic; -- for swap (0 for rdst, 1 for rsrc2)
  signal cu_reg_write_en  : std_logic;

  -- Mux/Logic Outputs
  signal s_selected_dest_addr : std_logic_vector(2 downto 0);
  signal s_sign_ext_imm       : std_logic_vector(31 downto 0);

  signal s_raw_imm_32 : std_logic_vector(31 downto 0); -- either from instruction or data bus

  signal s_jmp_type         : jmp_t;
  signal s_is_branch        : std_logic;
  signal s_mem_to_pc_enable : std_logic;

  signal s_jmp_take           : std_logic;
  signal s_is_swap : std_logic;

begin

  -- instruction splitting
  s_opcode    <= if_id_instruction(31 downto 27);
  s_rdst_idx  <= if_id_instruction(26 downto 24);
  s_rsrc1_idx <= if_id_instruction(23 downto 21);
  s_rsrc2_idx <= if_id_instruction(20 downto 18);
  -- s_imm_16    <= if_id_instruction(15 downto 0);

  -- for forwarding unit
  id_ex_rs1_addr <= s_rsrc1_idx;
  id_ex_rs2_addr <= s_rsrc2_idx;

  id_ex_pc_plus_1 <= if_id_pc_plus_1;

  u_control_unit : entity work.control_unit
    port map (
      clk         => clk,
      rst         => rst,
      intr        => intr,
      stall_fetch => stall_fetch,
      opcode      => s_opcode,

      -- EX Controls
      alu_op       => id_ex_alu_op,
      ccr_write_en => id_ex_ccr_write_en,
      ccr_src_sel  => id_ex_ccr_src_sel,

      -- MEM Controls
      mem_read     => id_ex_mem_read,
      mem_write    => id_ex_mem_write,
      sp_enable    => id_ex_sp_enable,
      push_pop     => id_ex_push_pop,
      mem_data_src => id_ex_mem_data_src,

      -- WB Controls
      reg_write_en  => cu_reg_write_en,
      reg_write_sel => cu_reg_write_sel,
      wb_source     => id_ex_wb_source,
      -- ccr_enable    => id_ex_ccr_enable,

      -- Flow Controls
      jmp_type           => id_ex_jmp_type,
      is_branch          => id_ex_is_branch,
      mem_to_pc_enable   => id_ex_mem_to_pc_enable,
      is_immediate_instr => id_ex_is_imm_instr,

      -- I/O
      is_out_port => id_ex_is_out_port,
      is_in_port  => id_ex_is_in_port,

      jmp_take           => s_jmp_take,
      is_swap => s_is_swap
    );

  id_ex_reg_write_en <= cu_reg_write_en;

  -- register right address mux
  -- 0: normal (Rdst bits 26:24)
  -- 1: in swap second cycle (Rsrc bits 23:21)
  id_ex_rd_addr <= s_rdst_idx when cu_reg_write_sel = '0' else
                   s_rsrc2_idx;

  -- register file instantiation
  u_reg_file : entity work.register_file
    port map (
      clk      => clk,
      rst      => rst,
      w_enable => wb_reg_write_en,
      addr_w   => wb_dest_addr,
      data_w   => wb_write_data,

      addr_1 => s_rsrc1_idx,
      addr_2 => s_rsrc2_idx,
      out_1  => id_ex_rdata1,
      out_2  => id_ex_rdata2
    );

  -- immediate mux and sign-extend
  -- For JMP/CALL: immediate is in next memory word (on data bus)
  -- For others: use instruction bits 15:0
  s_raw_imm_32 <= data_bus_in(31 downto 0) when (s_opcode = op_jmp or s_opcode = op_call) else
                  if_id_instruction(31 downto 0);
                  
  -- s_sign_ext_imm <= std_logic_vector(resize(signed(s_raw_imm_16), 32));

  immediate <= s_raw_imm_32; -- to ID/EX (for ALU)

  jmp_take_out <= s_jmp_take;

  is_swap_out <= s_is_swap;

end architecture structural;

@@ -75,28 +75,15 @@ begin
     port map (
       clk                      => clk,
       rst                      => rst,
-      wb_reg_source_in         => wb_reg_source_in,
-      mem_write_enable_in      => mem_write_enable_in,
-      mem_read_enable_in       => mem_read_enable_in,
-      write_back_enable_in     => write_back_enable_in,
       sp_bit_in                => sp_bit_in,
       push_pop_select_in       => push_pop_select_in,
       mem_write_data_select_in => mem_write_data_select_in,
-      write_addr_in            => write_addr_in,
       alu_result_in            => alu_result_in,
       reg_data_2_in            => reg_data_2_in,
       pc_plus_2_in             => pc_plus_2_in,
       ccr_flags_in             => ccr_flags_in,
       mem_address              => mem_address,
-      mem_write_data           => mem_write_data,
-      mem_write_en             => mem_write_en,
-      mem_read_data            => mem_read_data,
-      wb_reg_source_out        => wb_reg_source_out,
-      write_back_enable_out    => write_back_enable_out,
-      write_addr_out           => write_addr_out,
-      alu_result_out           => alu_result_out,
-      mem_read_data_out        => mem_read_data_out,
-      sp_out                   => sp_out
+      mem_write_data           => mem_write_data
     );
 
   stim_proc : process
@@ -175,13 +162,13 @@ begin
     -- First disable sp_bit to stop SP from changing
     sp_bit_in <= '0';
     wait for CLK_PERIOD;
-    
+
     -- Capture current SP value, then enable PUSH
     sp_bit_in          <= '1';
     push_pop_select_in <= push_op;
     wait for CLK_PERIOD;  -- SP decrements on this edge
     wait for 1 ns;        -- Small delay to see result
-    
+
     -- SP should have decremented by 1 from previous value
     -- After all the previous tests with sp_bit_in='1', SP has changed
     -- Let's just check it's less than sp_init
@@ -193,7 +180,7 @@ begin
     push_pop_select_in <= pop_op;
     wait for CLK_PERIOD;  -- SP increments on this edge
     wait for 1 ns;
-    
+
     -- Just verify SP changed (incremented)
     check_result(pass_count, fail_count, unsigned(sp_out) <= unsigned(sp_init), "SP incremented back");

examples.patch:
@@ -41,79 +41,71 @@ begin
     -- TEST CASE 1: ADD
     src_a   <= x"00000005";                                                              -- 5
     src_b   <= x"00000003";                                                              -- 3
-    alu_sel <= "000";                                                                    -- ADD
+    alu_sel <= alu_add;                                                                    -- ADD
     wait for 10 ns;
     assert result = x"00000008"
       report "Error: ADD failed. Expected 00000008, got " & to_hstring(result)
       severity error;
     -- TEST CASE 2: SUB
     src_a   <= x"00000005";                                                              -- 5
     src_b   <= x"00000003";                                                              -- 3
-    alu_sel <= "001";                                                                    -- SUB
+    alu_sel <= alu_sub;                                                                    -- SUB
     wait for 10 ns;
     assert result = x"00000002"
       report "Error: SUB failed. Expected 00000002, got " & to_hstring(result)
       severity error;
     -- TEST CASE 3: INC
     src_a   <= x"00000005";                                                              -- 5
     src_b   <= x"00000003";                                                              -- 3
-    alu_sel <= "010";                                                                    -- INC
+    alu_sel <= alu_inc;                                                                    -- INC
     wait for 10 ns;
     assert result = x"00000006"
       report "Error: INC failed. Expected 00000006, got " & to_hstring(result)
       severity error;
     -- TEST CASE 4: NOT
     src_a   <= x"00000005";                                                              -- 5
     src_b   <= x"00000003";                                                              -- 3
-    alu_sel <= "100";                                                                    -- NOT
+    alu_sel <= alu_not;                                                                    -- NOT
     wait for 10 ns;
     assert result = x"FFFFFFFA"
       report "Error: NOT failed. Expected FFFFFFFA, got " & to_hstring(result)
       severity error;
     -- TEST CASE 5: AND
     src_a   <= x"00000005";                                                              -- 5
     src_b   <= x"00000003";                                                              -- 3
-    alu_sel <= "110";                                                                    -- AND
+    alu_sel <= alu_and;                                                                    -- AND
     wait for 10 ns;
     assert result = x"00000001"
       report "Error: AND failed. Expected 00000001, got " & to_hstring(result)
       severity error;
-    -- TEST CASE 6: PASS
+    -- TEST CASE 6: PASS A
     src_a   <= x"00000005";                                                              -- 5
     src_b   <= x"00000003";                                                              -- 3
-    alu_sel <= "101";                                                                    -- PASS
+    alu_sel <= alu_pas_a;                                                                    -- PASS
     wait for 10 ns;
     assert result = x"00000005"
       report "Error: PASS failed. Expected 00000005, got " & to_hstring(result)
       severity error;
-    -- TEST CASE 7: DEC
-    src_a   <= x"00000005";                                                              -- 5
-    src_b   <= x"00000003";                                                              -- 3
-    alu_sel <= "011";                                                                    -- DEC
-    wait for 10 ns;
-    assert result = x"00000004"
-      report "Error: DEC failed. Expected 00000004, got " & to_hstring(result)
-      severity error;
-    -- TEST CASE 8: flags check for zero result
+    -- TEST CASE 7: flags check for zero result
     src_a   <= x"00000003";                                                              -- 3
     src_b   <= x"00000003";                                                              -- 3
-    alu_sel <= "001";                                                                    -- SUB
+    alu_sel <= alu_sub;                                                                    -- SUB
     wait for 10 ns;
     assert flags(0) = '1'
       report "Error: Zero flag failed. Expected 1, got " & std_logic'image(flags(0))
       severity error;
-    -- TEST CASE 9: flags check for negative result
+    -- TEST CASE 8: flags check for negative result
     src_a   <= x"00000002";                                                              -- 2
     src_b   <= x"00000003";                                                              -- 3
-    alu_sel <= "001";                                                                    -- SUB
+    alu_sel <= alu_sub;                                                                    -- SUB
     wait for 10 ns;
     assert flags(1) = '1'
       report "Error: Negative flag failed. Expected 1, got " & std_logic'image(flags(1))
       severity error;
-    -- TEST CASE 10: flags check for carry out
+    -- TEST CASE 9: flags check for carry out
     src_a   <= x"FFFFFFFF";                                                              -- Max unsigned
     src_b   <= x"00000001";                                                              -- 1
-    alu_sel <= "000";                                                                    -- ADD
+    alu_sel <= alu_add;                                                                    -- ADD
     wait for 10 ns;
     assert flags(2) = '1'
       report "Error: Carry flag failed. Expected 1, got " & std_logic'image(flags(2))
